value.found_quote: When Claude writes a workflow for a task you'll repeat, you can save that run's script as a command. A process like a review you run on every branch then runs the same orchestration each time... Press Enter to save. The workflow runs as `/<name>` in future sessions from either location.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes a "Workflows" feature in Claude Code that allows users to define repeatable, named multi-step task sequences. Specifically, it covers: (1) workflows as scripts that orchestrate multi-step agent tasks, (2) the ability to save a workflow run's script as a named command (e.g., `/<name>`) for reuse, (3) these saved workflows appear in autocomplete and can be rerun in future sessions. This directly substantiates the claim of "Workflows / saved automations" that define "repeatable, named multi-step task sequences." The feature is described as generally available on all paid plans (Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise) and requires Claude Code v2.1.154 or later, indicating active/GA status.
